data.title: Price predictions 10/3: BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, DOGE, ADA, HYPE, LINK, SUI
data.description:
Key points:

  • Solid inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs show that bulls are back in the driver’s seat and a rally to a new all-time high is likely.

  • BNB is leading the altcoin recovery, with several altcoins poised to break above their overhead resistance levels.

Bitcoin rose close to $123,900 on Friday, continuing its march toward the all-time high of $124,474. BTC’s recovery is backed by solid demand from the bulls, and the US spot BTC exchange-traded funds recorded $2.25 billion in inflows since Monday, according to Farside Investors data.

Analysts expect BTC to surge to a new all-time high. Capriole Investments founder Charles Edwards told Cointelegraph that BTC could skyrocket to $150,000 before the end of the year as investors seek safe-haven investments alongside gold.

While all signs point to a possible continuation of the uptrend, some analysts are cautious. Trader Roman said in a post on X that the relative strength index (RSI) indicator on BTC’s chart is exhibiting a bearish divergence on both the weekly and monthly time frames. Roman cautioned traders to be “careful holding here.”

Could BTC soar to a new all-time high, triggering a rally in altcoins? Let’s analyze the charts of the top 10 cryptocurrencies to find out.

Bitcoin price prediction

BTC surged above the $117,500 overhead resistance on Wednesday, indicating that the buyers are back in command.

The pair has reached near the all-time high of $124,474, where the bears are expected to mount a strong defense. If the price turns down sharply from the current level of $124,474 and breaks below $117,500, it signals that the bears are active at higher levels. The Bitcoin price may then remain between $107,000 and $124,474 for a while longer.

Instead, if buyers drive the price above $124,474, it signals the resumption of the uptrend. The pair may then rally to $141,948.

Ether price prediction

Ether (ETH) closed above the 20-day exponential moving average ($4,309) on Wednesday and reached the resistance line on Friday.

The 20-day EMA has started to turn up gradually, and the RSI has risen into the positive territory, signaling a slight edge to the bulls. Sellers will attempt to halt the recovery at the resistance line, but if the buyers prevail, the pair could retest the all-time high at $4,957.

The bears will have to pull the price below the 20-day EMA to weaken the bullish momentum. The Ether price could then drop to $4,060.

XRP price prediction

Buyers pushed XRP (XRP) above the downtrend line on Thursday but were unable to achieve a close above it.

Buyers are again attempting to maintain the XRP price above the downtrend line. If they succeed, the bearish descending triangle pattern will be invalidated. The pair may then climb to $3.20 and later to $3.38.

This optimistic view will be negated in the near term if the price turns down and breaks below the moving averages. That suggests the breakout above the downtrend line may have been a bull trap. 

BNB price prediction

BNB (BNB) skyrocketed to a new all-time high above $1,084 on Thursday and extended the up move on Friday.

The pair has broken above the ascending channel pattern, signaling a pickup in bullish momentum. There is minor resistance at $1,173, but if this level is crossed, the rally could extend to $1,252.

The breakout level from the channel and the 20-day EMA ($1,004) are likely to act as strong supports on the downside. Sellers will have to drag the BNB price below $930 to suggest that the pair may have topped out in the short term.

Solana price prediction

Buyers pushed Solana (SOL) back above the uptrend line on Wednesday, suggesting that the corrective phase may be over.

Any pullback from the current level is likely to find support at the 20-day EMA ($220). If that happens, the pair could rally to the overhead resistance of $260. Sellers are expected to defend the $260 level with all their might because a close above it could catapult the Solana price to $295.

Sellers will have to tug the price below the 50-day simple moving average ($212) to make a comeback.

Dogecoin price prediction

Dogecoin (DOGE) closed above the 20-day EMA ($0.24) on Wednesday, indicating a slight edge to the bulls.

Although the pair remains stuck inside a large range between $0.14 and $0.29, the price action is forming an ascending triangle pattern. Buyers will have to achieve a close above $0.29 to complete the bullish setup. DOGE may then rally to the pattern target of $0.39.

The bullish pattern will be invalidated if the bears pull the price below the uptrend line. That suggests the pair may extend its consolidation for some more time.

Cardano price prediction

Cardano’s (ADA) recovery rose above the 50-day SMA ($0.86) on Thursday, indicating that the selling pressure is reducing.

Buyers will have to propel the Cardano price above the resistance line to suggest that the correction may be over. The pair could then attempt a rally to $1.02, where the bears are expected to step in.

Contrarily, if the price turns down from the current level or the resistance line and breaks below the 20-day EMA ($0.84), it suggests that the bears are selling on rallies. The pair may then slump to the $0.75 support.

Hyperliquid price prediction

Hyperliquid (HYPE) surged above the moving averages on Thursday, indicating solid buying at lower levels.

The relief rally is expected to face selling at the 61.8% Fibonacci retracement level of $51.87. If the price turns down from $51.87 but bounces off the moving averages, it suggests that the sentiment has turned bullish. The HYPE/USDT pair could then ascend to $59.41.

On the contrary, if the price turns down and breaks below the moving averages, it signals that the bears are active at higher levels. The Hyperliquid price could then tumble to $43 and thereafter to $39.68.

Chainlink price prediction

Chainlink (LINK) rose above the 20-day EMA ($22.35) on Wednesday, but the bulls are facing resistance near the downtrend line.

If the price skids and remains below the 20-day EMA, it suggests that the pair could stay inside the descending channel pattern for a few more days.

The first sign of strength will be a break and close above the downtrend line. If that happens, the Chainlink price could rally to $26 and, after that, to $27. Sellers will attempt to halt the up move at $27, but if the bulls prevail, the rally could reach $30.94.

Sui price prediction

Sui (SUI) climbed above the moving averages on Wednesday, indicating that the selling pressure is reducing.

If buyers maintain the price above the moving averages, the pair could climb to the downtrend line. Sellers are expected to defend the downtrend line aggressively because a break above it could propel the Sui price to $4.20 and subsequently to $4.44.

On the contrary, if the price turns down and breaks below the moving averages, it suggests that the bears have not given up. The pair may then tumble to the $3.26 to $3.06 support zone.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

Novo plans online launch for obesity pill once approved, Bloomberg News reports
data.description: Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk said it plans to make its new obesity pill available through telehealth platforms like Ro and WeightWatchers once it gets U.S. approval, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

data.title: Brazil's Ambipar plunges over 50%, extending sell-off amid debt woes
data.description:
** Shares in Brazilian waste management company Ambipar plunge more than 50% in midday trading on Friday, heading for its fourth-straight session of losses

** Ambipar announced last week it had secured a preliminary court injunction, temporarily preventing the accelerated maturity of its debt

** The court ruling comes amid a dispute with Deutsche Bank , which had demanded additional collateral for loans extended to the company

** Following the announcement, analysts at UBS warned that "the acceleration of one creditor could trigger a cascade across all contracts, pushing Ambipar into technical default and halting operations"

** Ambipar has tapped BR Partners as financial adviser, with a source telling Reuters Ambipar is looking to renegotiate outstanding debt to avoid bankruptcy proceedings

** Ambipar's shares are down 56.4% in the session to about 1.2 reais ($0.23) each, while Brazil's benchmark stock index Bovespa gains 0.2%

** Ambipar's shares fell nearly 90% in the last four sessions

($1 = 5.3385 reais)

data.title: Government Shutdown Unlikely to Disturb Municipal Bonds — Market Talk
data.description:
1319 ET - The federal government shutdown is unlikely to be a major event for municipal bond issuers, Thornburg Investment Management says in a note. However, certain areas are more exposed "in terms of federal workers, federal contracts and federal spending on goods and services," the firm says. Such places, including Virginia, Maryland and D.C., could see a temporary hit on tax revenue due to deferred wages. Thornburg adds that sales taxes could also suffer. If the shutdown extends past 30 days, federal funding for food stamps could be threatened "and states would likely have to tap their own funds to cover the gap," the firm says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1226 ET - These are risky times to jump into long-term Treasury bonds. The expected Fed cut this month won't weaken long-term yields, Harbor Capital's Jake Schurmeier says. Because inflation is still above the Fed's 2% target, rate cuts risk boosting consumer prices. Economic activity is forecast to remain relatively strong, further stoking inflation risks, Schurmeier says. All that makes interest rate hikes more likely in the long run, a reason why yields on Treasurys maturing in 10 years or more are falling much slower than in shorter maturities. As of yesterday, the 10-year yield was down 0.488 percentage point this year, compared to 0.703 p.p. for the two-year. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1216 ET - Alaska Air and American Airlines will be among the most challenged carriers to emerge from the third-quarter earnings season, analysts say. Both are expected to be the only airlines to report a decline in earnings from the same period a year earlier, Deutsche Bank analysts say, adding that inclement weather at key hubs in Seattle and Dallas hurt both carriers. TD Cowen analysts expect Alaska and American will revise fourth-quarter estimates down. The analysts are confident about the two carriers' long-term performance, but think they have less near-term upside than some of their rivals. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1202 ET - The airline industry still looks like a tale of two tiers as companies prepare to report their third-quarter financials, TD Cowen analysts say. United Airlines and Delta Air Lines are poised to continue leading the pack, as they are more exposed to improving corporate travel and long-haul international demand, the analysts say. They also have better-performing loyalty programs and durable demand for premium seating. Airlines that rely more on domestic travel demand will be more challenged in the near-term because pricing for main cabin seats and domestic flights remains soft, the analysts say. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1056 ET - The credit spreads on euro-denominated corporate bonds could tighten in the coming weeks as new issuance remains low, Societe Generale's Juan Valencia says in a note. New supply of euro corporate bonds is expected to remain muted during the earnings blackout period throughout October, he says. "We may well see spreads improve further, as anecdotal evidence shows that there is still cash to put to work and the recent heavy new issue volumes have failed to satiate demand for credit." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0929 ET - Oil prices try to recover some of the ground lost this week, as markets brace for increased OPEC+ production. The potential for added supply is combined with concerns that a slowing U.S. economy could dent demand. Meanwhile, a fire at Chevron's El Segundo refinery in Los Angeles has been contained, according to local police. Still, the incident helps lift prices. WTI rises 0.4% to $60.75 a barrel. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0914 ET - Brazilian corporations could soon find it harder to get affordable financing, according to Fitch Ratings. The firm says in a report that about 23% of local market debt in its portfolio was due to mature in 2025-2026. Strong liquidity has allowed companies to refinance debt in past months, with Fitch-rated entities issuing more than 39 billion Brazilian reais ($7.3 billion) in 3Q, the firm says. However, recent credit events, including downgrades, creates "the potential for a shift toward quality that could tighten conditions for weaker credits," Fitch says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0909 ET - Bitcoin could rise to a fresh all-time high next week if the U.S. government shutdown persists, Standard Chartered's Geoff Kendrick says in a note. This year the cryptocurrency has traded alongside U.S. government risks, with a positive correlation to the 10-year Treasury term premium, or yield investors demand for holding these bonds, he says. Polymarket shows odds of 60% the shutdown will last between 10-29 days so a near-term conclusion seems unlikely. "I suspect Bitcoin will rise throughout the shutdown." StanChart expects bitcoin to rise to $135,000 in the near term and $200,000 by year-end. Bitcoin falls 0.2% to $120,456 after reaching a seven-week high of $121,065 Thursday, LSEG data show. It hit a record high of $124,480 in mid-August.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0904 ET - Issuance of new euro-denominated corporate bonds fell modestly this week, but supply remains strong, Societe Generale's Juan Valencia says in a note. New issues amounted to 14 billion euros ($16.4 billion), excluding Friday's transactions, lower than last week's total of 26.6 billion euros, he says. "[Primary market] activity remains healthy despite a clear drop in volumes," he said. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0848 ET - Bond markets are lukewarm, as is the dollar, as Jobs Friday turns into a data void with the U.S. government shutdown delaying the September payrolls release. Economists surveyed by WSJ were expecting job creation to increase to 51,000 from 22,000, with unemployment steady at 4.3%. Other labor statistics earlier this week pointed to continued weakness in hiring amid contained layoffs. The September ISM report on business services PMI, put out by a not-for-profit professional supply management group, and due at 10 a.m. ET, is forecast to repeat August's 52 level. The 10-year yield is at 4.085% and the two-year at 3.545%. The WSJ Dollar Index is flat. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0841 ET - A prolonged U.S. government shutdown could cause a modest slowdown in economic growth, Fitch Ratings says in a note. "A protracted disruption, particularly if accompanied by significant funding withdrawals or workforce reductions, could slightly slow U.S. economic growth." In the near term, however, the shutdown is expected to have limited impact on the economy, Fitch Ratings says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0707 ET - Oil prices could fall as OPEC+ is expected to agree to a production increase during the weekend, but the implications for the currency market look unclear, MUFG Bank's Derek Halpenny says in a note. With the U.S. a significant crude oil producer, a decline in prices can coincide with a weaker dollar--especially if the price decline is supply increase related, he says. In the case of a demand shock that dents risk sentiment, the dollar could benefit from safe haven flows. For the euro, lower oil prices would be positive from a real incomes perspective. If the drop in crude oil was large enough, however, it could give the European Central Bank scope to cut interest rates again, he says. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Soligenix CEO Christopher J. Schaber Acquires 15,132 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameSchaber Christopher J
RelationshipCHAIRMAN, CEO AND PRESIDENT
TypePurchase
Amount$19,999
SEC FilingForm 4

Christopher J. Schaber, Chairman, CEO, and President of Soligenix, has purchased 15,132 shares of common stock at a weighted-average price of $1.3217 per share, totaling $19,999. Following this transaction, Schaber directly owns 15,511 shares of Soligenix common stock.

SEC Filing: SOLIGENIX, INC. [ SNGX ] - Form 4 - Oct. 03, 2025

data.title: Mali army holds back 70 Allied Gold trucks as militants block fuel imports
data.description:
By Portia Crowe

Mali's military has prevented about 70 fuel trucks from travelling to Allied Gold's Sadiola mine after al Qaeda-linked militants imposed a blockade on fuel imports to the landlocked country, two people familiar with the matter said.

Fuel supplies are dwindling at the remote gold mine, located some 650 km (400 miles) from the capital Bamako, they said.

Mali's military government, which took power after coups in 2020 and 2021, is facing growing pressure from militant groups who analysts say are trying to encircle cities and towns in the Sahel region.

"We are telling all traders who import diesel and gasoline into Mali, whether from Ivory Coast, Guinea, Senegal, or Mauritania, to stop doing so until further notice," a Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) militant spokesperson said in a video announcing the blockade in early September.

"Why? Because these bandits in power are persecuting people, closing their gas stations, and cutting off fuel to villagers under the pretext that they are supplying jihadists."

In recent weeks, armed forces have kept many of the fuel tankers destined for Sadiola in the town of Diboli on the border with Senegal, while several others are being held in the town of Kayes, about 75 km north of Sadiola, until soldiers can escort the trucks to the site, both sources said.

Three tankers managed to reach the site under military escort this week, one of the sources said.

In Mali, companies can sometimes wait weeks or months to secure military escorts due to limited availability.

Spokespeople for Allied Gold and for the Malian military did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

Last month, at least 40 fuel tankers were destroyed when insurgents attacked a convoy of more than 100 vehicles under military escort heading for Bamako.

In May, militants attacked a convoy transporting heavy mining equipment from Bamako to Sadiola, underscoring widening security risks facing mining companies operating in a military-led country struggling to contain jihadist groups.
